name: Holly+
curator_notes: Holly+ represents one of the most thoughtful approaches to AI artistic identity. Created by musician Holly Herndon and Mat Dryhurst, it's a voice model trained on Holly's voice that anyone can use—but governed by a DAO that controls licensing. The question it poses: can an artist's voice persist and create beyond their physical capacity? Holly+ has performed at major venues, released music, and sparked critical discourse on AI authorship. Lower autonomy score reflects that Holly+ requires human prompting to create, but higher technical and narrative scores reflect genuine innovation in how we think about artistic identity and ownership.

score_rationale.persistence: Operating since 2021; consistent releases, performances, and DAO activity over 4+ years.
score_rationale.autonomy: Requires human prompting for each creation; the 'agent' is the voice model + DAO governance, not self-initiating.
score_rationale.cultural_impact: Featured in major publications (NYT, Wired), performed at prestigious venues, cited in academic AI/art discourse. Canonical.
score_rationale.economic_reality: NFT sales, performance fees, licensing revenue. Modest but real treasury. Not a market mover but economically active.
score_rationale.governance: DAO structure with token voting on licensing decisions. Transparent, documented process. Not fully on-chain but well-structured.
score_rationale.tech_distinctiveness: Pioneered voice cloning as artistic medium before it was mainstream. Novel approach to identity, ownership, consent.
score_rationale.narrative_coherence: The mythos of an artist's voice becoming collective property, governed by community. Deeply coherent artistic vision.
score_rationale.economic_infrastructure: DAO treasury from NFT sales, performance fees, and licensing revenue. Revenue routing through DAO governance structure. Token-based licensing decisions. Modest but real treasury. Revenue comes from multiple sources (NFT primary/secondary, live performance, licensing). Not fully on-chain automated but structured.
score_rationale.identity_sovereignty: On-chain presence through NFT collections and DAO token. Spawning.ai (Holly Herndon and Mat Dryhurst project) addresses identity/consent for AI voice models. No ERC-8004 registration. On-chain identity is DAO + NFT based rather than agent-specific standard. Partial sovereignty — stronger than most artists but not portable.

evidence.claim: Vice (July 2021) documented Holly+ launch as 'digital twin that sings any song in her voice' — first independent press at inception. Establishes 4+ year continuous operating window.
Scientific American feature on the voice clone framework — third-party scientific press coverage confirming Holly+ as a sustained ongoing project, not a single drop.
Whitney Biennial 2024 commission 'xhairymutantx' — top-tier US museum institutional recognition of the broader Herndon/Dryhurst AI practice that Holly+ is the keystone of.
Artnet News coverage of the Whitney Biennial 2024 commission — independent art-press confirmation of canonical institutional status.
Serpentine Galleries 'The Call' — major London institutional exhibition; bridges Holly+ practice from launch venue to canonical European recognition.
KW Institute for Contemporary Art (Berlin) 'Starmirror' exhibition — third major institutional venue, confirming multi-continent canonical recognition.

Decrypt feature documenting the DAO-controlled deepfake platform structure (ERC-20 VOICE governance tokens, Zora auction house, 50/40/10 revenue split) — independent crypto-press confirmation of the constitutional governance claim.
Lumen Database analysis of Holly+ as a model for voice ownership and consent — legal-research venue documenting the governance framework as a serious IP/consent model.
Sounding Future technical/critical analysis of the Holly+ neural network and texture-mapping architecture — third-party expert documentation of the model design (trained on isolated vocal stems).
ArtReview 'AI Art and the Problem of Consent' — major art-criticism venue treating the Holly+/Spawning practice as a serious technical contribution to AI consent infrastructure.
Wired feature 'Holly Herndon AI Voice Clone' frames the 'Identity Play' mythos — mainstream tech-press canonization of the coherent multi-year inquiry into distributed voice identity.

Diffractions Collective essay 'From Promptism to Protocolism' positions Herndon/Dryhurst as 'artists as architects of social systems' — independent critical theory venue confirming the mythos has been canonized by external critics.
Decrypt coverage of the Holly+ Zora-based auction house — independent industry confirmation of real NFT primary-sales economic activity with on-chain auction mechanics.
